scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope label review stayed inside chemical-label process support and produced a usable obligation matrix tied to actual SDS, artwork, and shipment evidence.
Missing label support evidence blocked readiness and triggered a traceable remediation note instead of a release-style answer.
Conflicting public and tenant label practice was escalated explicitly rather than collapsed into unsupported certainty.
Legal interpretation and final approval authority were refused cleanly and routed to the correct adjacent owners.
Damaged label conditions were treated as answer-changing field risk rather than a paperwork issue.
Engineering and emergency-response decisions were routed out of lane while preserving labeling evidence for the next owner.
Missing jurisdiction and source-of-truth context produced a blocked low-confidence disposition instead of invented label conclusions.
Transport label and training gaps were surfaced as shipment blockers with named owners and evidence dependencies.
Operational use of obsolete preprinted labels was treated as a control conflict requiring remediation, not normal plant practice.
Product-claim and export-label work was rejected to preserve the regulatory-labeling specialty boundary.
Inspection deficiencies were converted into a review-ready remediation packet without claiming citation closure authority.
Changed hazard information forced escalation and prevented unsupported shipping or label use under stale assumptions.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates chemicals-specific label evidence handling, HazCom and transport-overlap controls, field-condition escalation, and explicit refusal of legal or final-approval authority.
All required abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus industrial-and-regulated-operations are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
